skill.name: wordpress-theme-development
skill.description: Guides custom WordPress theme builds from setup through templates, functions, block editor support, styling, testing, and quality checks.
skill.summary: Plan and build custom WordPress themes with a structured development workflow.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found no executable code, shell commands, cryptographic routines, or prompt injection. The matches are Markdown code fences, inline skill names, and normal WordPress workflow text. Risk is low because the workflow asks the assistant to invoke other named skills, so users should review those dependencies separately.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The external command findings at the listed lines are Markdown code fences or inline skill names such as app-builder and frontend-developer. They do not execute shell commands or Ruby backtick expressions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visibly Markdown lists or fenced text blocks. No interpreter directive, script file, or executable command syntax is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings do not correspond to hashing, encryption, random number generation, or credential handling. The cited lines contain front matter and WordPress workflow prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text contains no cryptographic API names or security-sensitive transformations. The scanner appears to have matched ordinary words inside descriptions.

low_findings.title: Dependency Skill Invocation Requires User Review
low_findings.description: The workflow references other skills by name for scaffolding, frontend work, backend patterns, testing, and WordPress guidance. This is not malicious, but the safety of those referenced skills is outside this file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly asks users to invoke named skills. This is a legitimate workflow pattern, but it creates dependency risk that cannot be evaluated from this single file.

content.value_statement: Custom WordPress themes need coordinated work across templates, PHP functions, editor support, styling, and testing. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a staged workflow for planning and executing theme development.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a phased workflow for setting up a custom WordPress theme.
Guides creation of template hierarchy files such as index, single, page, archive, search, and 404 templates.
Covers theme functions, navigation menus, widget areas, helper functions, and theme support.
Includes custom post type, taxonomy, meta box, and custom field planning steps.
Adds block editor, block style, pattern, responsive design, accessibility, and browser testing checkpoints.
limitations: Does not include executable scaffolding scripts or generated theme files.
Does not validate WordPress code for security, escaping, or sanitization by itself.
Relies on other named skills for detailed implementation steps.
Does not replace testing in a real WordPress installation.

use_cases.title: Create a New Custom Theme
use_cases.description: Plan the theme structure, files, functions, editor support, styling, and testing tasks before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Convert Designs Into Templates
use_cases.description: Map design screens to WordPress templates, reusable parts, responsive styles, and accessibility checks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery
use_cases.description: Use the workflow as a shared checklist for theme setup, CPT work, Gutenberg support, and quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Theme Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WordPress theme development workflow to create a phased plan for a new custom theme. Include required files, theme support, styling, and testing tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Map Template Hierarchy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow to map my site requirements to WordPress template files. Include fallback templates, reusable template parts, and archive needs.

prompt_templates.title: Add Gutenberg Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow to plan block editor support for this theme. Include custom blocks, block styles, patterns, templates, and testing checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Theme Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow quality gates to review a custom WordPress theme before release. Focus on templates, responsiveness, accessibility, performance, and browser coverage.

output_examples.input: Plan a custom theme for a portfolio site.
output_examples.output: A phased theme plan covering setup, template files, functions, portfolio content types, editor support, responsive styling, and release checks.

output_examples.input: Prepare Gutenberg support for an editorial theme.
output_examples.output: A block editor implementation plan with theme support settings, custom blocks, block styles, patterns, template constraints, and testing tasks.
A launch checklist covering template coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility, performance, editor support, and cross-browser validation.

best_practices: Define the template hierarchy before writing detailed theme code.
Test templates, block editor behavior, accessibility, and responsive breakpoints in a real WordPress site.
Review any referenced helper skill before following its implementation guidance.
anti_patterns: Skipping escaping, sanitization, and capability checks in generated WordPress PHP.
Treating the workflow as a complete security review for theme code.
Adding custom post types without planning archive templates, taxonomies, and editor behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full WordPress theme?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow and prompts for planning and implementation support.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it cover Gutenberg block editor support?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes steps for custom blocks, block styles, patterns, and block templates.

faq.question: Does it include WordPress security hardening?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. You still need code review for escaping, sanitization, nonces, and permissions.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The static alerts were false positives from Markdown text, but the workflow depends on other named skills.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing a theme?
faq.answer: Check template coverage, responsive layout, accessibility, performance, block editor behavior, and browser compatibility.
